Module 3:
Quick Launch, Part 3
!
Executive Summary
Example 2: “Go Romance” - This example a bit more complex
• 	Timing
• 	9 emails - plus the blog and a landing page
• 	2 weeks
• 	Offer
• 	Could be a bundle of digital products
• 	Discount off normal price - ex: $350 value for $97
• 	In actuality, by bundling it ends up being the highest price ever for
this website in the example
• 	Triggers
• 	Reason why
• 	Story
• 	Scarcity
• 	Event-based
!
1	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 1 of 5 --

• 	Community-based
• 	Likeability
• 	Conversation
• 	Anticipation
• 	Emotion
• 	Email 1
• 	Triggers: story, likability (shades of reluctant hero)
• 	My client was sharing a personal story about daughter getting
married and wrote that she was nervous about sharing - this instantly
brings people in
• 	“Just press the reply button in your email right now” - this gets a
much bigger response than you can even imagine
• 	The email tells a story and creates a lot of likeability
• 	Email 2
• 	Triggers: story, community, likeability, reciprocity, conversation, social
proof
• 	Email begins with gratitude for the 200 email responses from email 1
- creates conversation and builds the community
• 	Email 3
!
2	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 5 --

• 	Triggers: story, likeability, reciprocity, community, conversation
• 	Lots of consistent triggers but the entire thing is very event based
• 	Sends people to a blog - this creates huge social proof
• 	People see all the notes with all the love in them - ended up with
4-500 notes
• 	Email 4
• 	Triggers: story, reason why, community, conversation
• 	Brings it all full circle - is sent after the bridal shower
• 	Switches the theme and moves into a “thank you plan”
• 	Offers a huge special discount on the products - created an entire
bundle that didn’t exist before
• 	This email is the switch to the offer
• 	Email 5
• 	Triggers: story, reason why, community, conversation, scarcity,
anticipation
• 	Adds in a little reluctant hero, “took a little longer than I expected to
put this together”
• 	“Putting finishing touches on the offer”
!
3	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 3 of 5 --

• 	Special bonuses for the first 50 people - first time scarcity is brought
in
• 	Email 6
• 	Triggers: scarcity
• 	Announcement email: “Mother of all offers is ready” - go check it out
right away
• 	Use early bird bonuses
• 	Tip: When you’re going live with something you’ve spent a lot of time
building up to, make the email short and get them straight to the
order page
• 	Email 7
• 	Triggers: story, strong reason why, scarcity
• 	Refers back to the “thank you plan”
• 	Explains a reason why for the deadline (scarcity) - needs to close
down before the wedding
• 	Email 8
• 	Triggers: conversation, reason why, scarcity, social proof
• 	Reason why message - offers a FAQ
• 	Take the top objections to what you’re selling and answer them
!
4	© 2014 Internet Alchemy, Inc.
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 4 of 5 --

• 	This can be done through email, blog post, or video
• 	In the example, she answered the three primary questions
• 	Email 9
• 	End of the launch - 24 hour warning
• 	Always a big spike at the end so don’t forget to end with a warning
• 	Lots of scarcity and strong reason why
!
Key Takeaways
Use the “Go Romance” technique to tell a story and sell one or more
products
This technique creates a lot of emotion and builds community, social
proof, and fan love
Many different triggers can be used
The launch is longer and more involved but delivers massive results
!
